meta.title: Welcome & workspace
objectives: Orient to Cursor layout (Glass, chat, editor)
Open this training repo as workspace

timeline.label: Demo
timeline.action: Toggle **Glass** side panel — note chat + file tree

meta.practicePrompt: In Agent: List three panels you will use this course (editor, chat/agent, terminal). Keep answers under 5 bullets.

quiz.q: Best first step when starting a lesson?

L01.demoMarkdown:
# L01: Welcome & workspace

## Setup (30s)

- Open `cursor-training-baseline` in Cursor
- Run `python3 -m http.server 5173` from repo root
- Open http://localhost:5173/web/ in browser

## Demo (3m)

1. Toggle **Glass** side panel — note chat + file tree
2. Open **Command Palette** → search "Open Settings"
3. Split editor: `content/lessons/L01-welcome/DEMO.md` + web catalog
4. Pin the training repo — avoid editing home directory by mistake
5. Show **Agent** tab vs **Chat** tab labels

## Practice (1m)

```
In Agent: List three panels you will use this course (editor, chat/agent, terminal). Keep answers under 5 bullets.
```

## Recap (30s)

- You can run web + Cursor side-by-side
- DEMO.md is the instructor script
- Quiz locks in workspace vocabulary

Complete the quiz in the web player (need 2/3 to mark complete).

meta.title: Chat vs Agent
objectives: Choose Chat for Q&A vs Agent for multi-step edits
Know when to escalate to Agent

timeline.label: Demo
timeline.action: Ask **Chat**: "What does divide() do?" — no edits

meta.practicePrompt: Agent: Add a one-line comment above `add()` explaining it is for L02 practice. Do not change logic.

quiz.q: Which mode should implement a feature across files?
After Agent proposes a diff you should?

L02.demoMarkdown:
# L02: Chat vs Agent

## Setup (30s)

- Open `content/practice-sandbox/src/calculator.ts`
- Open web lesson L02 timer

## Demo (3m)

1. Ask **Chat**: "What does divide() do?" — no edits
2. Switch to **Agent**: "Add JSDoc to divide() only" — allow diff
3. Type `@calculator.ts` in prompt — confirm file in context
4. Reject one hunk, accept another — practice review
5. Compare token use: chat answer vs agent plan

## Practice (1m)

```
Agent: Add a one-line comment above `add()` explaining it is for L02 practice. Do not change logic.
```

## Recap (30s)

- Chat explains; Agent edits
- @ ties files into context

Complete the quiz in the web player (need 2/3 to mark complete).

meta.title: Inline edits & diffs
objectives: Use inline edit on a selection

timeline.label: Demo
timeline.action: Inline edit selection: "add overflow check comment"

meta.practicePrompt: Inline edit: rename local variable in `subtract` for clarity (a,b → minuend/subtrahend) — one function only.

L03.demoMarkdown:
# L03: Inline edits & diffs

## Setup (30s)

- Open `content/practice-sandbox/src/calculator.ts`
- Select the `multiply` function body

## Demo (3m)

1. Inline edit selection: "add overflow check comment"
2. Open diff view — read red/green hunks
3. Reject if change touches unrelated functions
4. Accept minimal hunk only
5. Save and verify file compiles

## Practice (1m)

```
Inline edit: rename local variable in `subtract` for clarity (a,b → minuend/subtrahend) — one function only.
```

## Recap (30s)

- Small selection → small diff
- Review before accept

Complete the quiz in the web player (need 2/3 to mark complete).

meta.title: Ask / Plan / Debug modes

timeline.label: Demo
timeline.action: **Debug**: "Why does sumPositive include negatives?" — trace logic

meta.practicePrompt: Debug mode: fix `parseCount` to return 0 for non-numeric input; add no new files.

L04.demoMarkdown:
# L04: Ask / Plan / Debug modes

## Setup (30s)

- Open `content/practice-sandbox/src/buggy.ts`
- Run: `node -e "import('./content/practice-sandbox/src/buggy.ts').then(m=>console.log(m.sumPositive([-1,2])))"

## Demo (3m)

1. **Debug**: "Why does sumPositive include negatives?" — trace logic
2. **Ask**: "Outline steps to fix without editing yet"
3. **Plan**: "Plan fix + test for sumPositive" — read plan before code
4. Apply smallest fix with Agent after plan approval
5. Re-run quick node check

## Practice (1m)

```
Debug mode: fix `parseCount` to return 0 for non-numeric input; add no new files.
```

## Recap (30s)

- Debug=repro
- Plan=big diff guard

Complete the quiz in the web player (need 2/3 to mark complete).

meta.title: Agent: multi-file task

meta.practicePrompt: Agent: add `mod(a,b)` with tests — max 2 files touched under practice-sandbox.

L06.demoMarkdown:
# L06: Agent: multi-file task

## Setup (30s)

- Open calculator.ts + calculator.test.ts
- Start L06 timer

## Demo (3m)

1. Agent prompt: "Implement safe divide() throwing on zero"
2. Point Agent to tests folder
3. Review diff across src + tests
4. Run `node --test content/practice-sandbox/tests/calculator.test.ts`
5. If fail, iterate once with log excerpt

## Practice (1m)

```
Agent: add `mod(a,b)` with tests — max 2 files touched under practice-sandbox.
```

## Recap (30s)

- Multi-file needs explicit paths
- Tests prove fix

Complete the quiz in the web player (need 2/3 to mark complete).

- Small commits
- Human review gate

meta.title: Browser automation
objectives: Use Browser MCP on localhost training app
meta.practicePrompt: Run semi-auto ASSIST or manually: open L18 lesson page and start timer.

L18.demoMarkdown:
# L18: Browser automation

## Setup (30s)

- Server at http://localhost:5173/web/
- Open L18 in web player
- Optional: enable cursor-ide-browser MCP

## Demo (3m)

1. browser_tabs list — confirm training URL
2. browser_navigate to catalog if needed
3. browser_snapshot — find lesson link ref
4. browser_click one lesson card (with lock workflow)
5. HANDOFF: learner clicks through quiz manually

## Practice (1m)

```
Run semi-auto ASSIST or manually: open L18 lesson page and start timer.
```

## Recap (30s)

- Only localhost training origin
- Max 4 retries then stop

Complete the quiz in the web player (need 2/3 to mark complete).
L18.assistMarkdown:
# Semi-auto assist — L18

**assistMode:** browser  
**Allowed origin:** http://localhost:5173 only (browser assist)

## Checkpoints

- 0:00 — Read ASSIST scope; confirm training repo open.
- 0:30 — HANDOFF_TO_LEARNER: you drive the first demo step in DEMO.md.
- 2:30 — Agent may run read-only/tool steps you approve.
- 4:30 — HANDOFF_TO_LEARNER: run practice prompt yourself.
- 5:00 — Stop all tools; quiz in web player.


## Browser MCP (allowlist only)

- Allowed origin: `http://localhost:5173` (training web app only)
- Workflow: browser_tabs list → browser_navigate → browser_lock → browser_snapshot → browser_click
- Max 4 retries then stop and HANDOFF_TO_LEARNER
- Never navigate outside localhost training URLs


## Task

You are demonstrating **Browser automation** for cursor-training-baseline.

1. Follow `content/lessons/L18-browser/DEMO.md` timeline.
2. Only edit files under `content/practice-sandbox/` unless DEMO says otherwise.
3. At each HANDOFF_TO_LEARNER, stop and wait for the learner.
4. If MCP/browser unavailable, tell learner to continue manually with DEMO.md.

## Practice handoff

Ask the learner to run:

```
Run semi-auto ASSIST or manually: open L18 lesson page and start timer.
```

Then stop.

L25.demoMarkdown:
# L25: Model & mode strategy

## Setup (30s)

- Open Settings → Models
- Recall L04 modes

## Demo (3m)

mcp.title: Model Context Protocol
mcp.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.
mcp.markdown:
# MCP (training excerpt)

MCP connects Cursor to external tools via servers and tool descriptors.

**Canonical docs:** https://cursor.com/docs/context/mcp

## Key concepts

- **Server** — configured integration (browser, GitLab, custom).
- **Tool descriptor** — JSON schema the agent reads before calling a tool.
- **Auth** — some servers require login or tokens.

## In Cursor

1. Open **Settings → MCP** (or project MCP folder).
2. Inspect enabled servers and tool names.
3. Before calling a tool, the agent should read the descriptor schema.

## Safety

Prefer narrow tool allowlists; never paste production secrets into prompts.

hooks.title: Cursor Hooks
hooks.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.
hooks.markdown:
# Cursor Hooks (training excerpt)

Hooks run scripts at lifecycle events so teams can audit, block, or reshape agent behavior.

**Canonical docs:** https://cursor.com/docs/agent/hooks

## Common events

| Event | Use |
|-------|-----|
| `afterFileEdit` | Audit or format after edits |
| `beforeShellExecution` | Block dangerous commands |
| `beforeMCPExecution` | Gate external tool calls |
| `beforeSubmitPrompt` | Redact secrets from prompts |
| `subagentStart` / `subagentStop` | Log delegated work |

## Configuration

`hooks.json` at project root lists matchers (glob) and command paths. Set `failClosed: true` on security gates so hook errors deny the action.

## Training tip

Start with one `afterFileEdit` audit hook, then add `beforeShellExecution` for production repos.

sdk-typescript.title: Cursor SDK (TypeScript)
sdk-typescript.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.

sdk-python.title: Cursor SDK (Python)
sdk-python.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.
sdk-python.markdown:
# Cursor SDK — Python (training excerpt)

Python parity with the TypeScript SDK for automation pipelines.

**Canonical docs:** https://cursor.com/docs/sdk/python

## One-shot

```python
from cursor_sdk import Agent

result = Agent.prompt(
    prompt="List open TODOs in practice-sandbox",
    cwd=".",
)
```

## Session

```python
session = Agent.create(prompt="Refactor calculator module")
session.resume(prompt="Add unit test for divide-by-zero")
```

## Notes

- Install via package docs for your environment.
- Keep `cwd` pointed at the repo you want the agent to edit.

cloud-agents.title: Cloud Agents
cloud-agents.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.
cloud-agents.markdown:
# Cloud Agents (training excerpt)

Run agents in Cursor's cloud runtime instead of your laptop.

**Canonical docs:** https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/api/endpoints

## When to use

- Long-running tasks without tying up local CPU
- CI or backend triggers that call `/v1/agents`
- Sharing a stable agent ID across resume calls

## IDs

Cloud agent IDs often start with `bc-`. Local sessions use workspace-scoped identifiers.

## REST pointer

Use the Cloud Agent API for create, prompt, resume, and status — see canonical endpoints table in live docs.

privacy.title: Privacy modes
privacy.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.
privacy.markdown:
# Privacy modes (training excerpt)

Controls what Cursor may store and what automations can persist.

**Canonical docs:** https://cursor.com/docs/account/privacy

## Modes (conceptual)

| Mode | Effect |
|------|--------|
| Default | Standard product telemetry and history per account settings |
| Stricter / team policies | Limit retention or sharing |
| `NO_STORAGE` (where available) | Avoid persisting automation outputs |

## Automations

Some triggers/actions require explicit storage consent. Gate backend MCP credentials per environment.

## Training tip

Document team choice in `AGENTS.md` so agents do not suggest flows that violate policy.

automations.title: Automations
automations.attribution: Curated training excerpt — see canonical URL.
automations.markdown:
# Automations (training excerpt)

Event-driven workflows inside Cursor (triggers → actions).

**Canonical docs:** https://cursor.com/docs/automations

## Mental model

1. **Trigger** — schedule, git event, manual, etc.
2. **Action** — run agent prompt, open Glass UI, call backend MCP.
3. **Prefill** — structured data passed to automation forms (not raw secrets in URLs).

## Backend MCP

Automations can call approved MCP servers for ticketing, deploys, or docs — keep allowlists tight.

## Privacy

Respect storage gates; automations that log customer data need review in L39.

manifest.attribution: Curated training excerpts for cursor-training-baseline. Canonical docs remain the source of truth on cursor.com.
